description: Adversarial invariant for the manual IVF and implantable-pacemaker sample audit: do not let later diagnostics, lithium-ion batteries, or hospital information systems become method prerequisites.

checks.reason: Standalone IVF predates and does not require molecular diagnostics or PGD workflows.
Hospital information systems are not part of the IVF laboratory method.
IVF requires controlled in vitro culture handling of eggs, sperm, and embryos.
Early implantable pacemakers predate lithium-ion batteries and should depend on general portable battery power instead.
Hospital information systems are monitoring/workflow infrastructure, not pacemaker device architecture.
Implantable pacemakers require electronic pulse-generator circuitry.
Implantable pacemakers require self-contained portable battery power.
Portable battery power should stay anchored to Volta's battery rather than later lithium-ion chronology.
Implantable pacemakers should stay anchored to the first human implant chronology.
IVF should stay anchored to the birth of Louise Brown, not a generated 1995 diagnostics date.
